---
title: "Data-Driven Insights into Movie Success and Trends"
subtitle: "Research Proposal Presentation"
author: "Group Name / Team Members"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, cpp.scss]   # <-- CPP custom theme file goes here (see note below)
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    incremental: true
    footer: "Movie Success & Trends | Data-Driven Insights"
---

## Team Introduction

**Data-Driven Insights into Movie Success and Trends**

- Team Member A
- Team Member B
- Team Member C
- Team Member D
- Team Member E

Presentation Date: `r Sys.Date()`

---

## Problem Definition

- Thousands of movies are released every year, but predicting **audience reception (ratings)** and **commercial success (popularity)** remains a major challenge.
- This matters deeply to studios, investors, and streaming platforms (OTT).

**Key Question:**
> Which factors (genre, language, release timing, engagement, etc.) influence a movie's popularity and rating?

---

## Importance of the Topic

**Target Audience**

- Movie studio marketing teams
- OTT content curators / streaming platforms

**Why It Matters**

- Helps identify high-potential content combinations under limited budgets
- Supports effective launch strategies targeted at the right audience segments

---

## Data & Population

**Dataset:** `mymoviedb.csv` (10,000+ movie metadata records)

**Key Variables**

- Numeric: `Popularity`, `Vote_Count`, `Vote_Average`
- Categorical / Text: `Title`, `Overview`, `Original_Language`, `Genre`, `Release_Date`

---

## Data Wrangling Plan

**From Records to Data**

- Load the CSV file into a data frame

**Tidying & Transforming**

- Split the `Genre` column (comma-separated string, e.g. "Action, Adventure") into individual genre rows
- Convert `Release_Date` string into a proper `Date` type
- Extract `Year`, `Month`, and `Season` variables from the date

::: {.notes}
Each team member owns one Research Objective (RO) for data wrangling and visualization.
:::

---

## RO1 — ABCDE {.smaller}

**(Team Member A)**

**Research Objective:** ABCDE

- **IV:** `ABCDE`
- **DV:** `ABCDE`, `

**Wrangling Plan:** ABCDE

**Visualization Plan:** ABCDE

---

## RO2 — ABCDE {.smaller}

**(Team Member B)**

**Research Objective:** ABCDE

- **IV:** `ABCDE` (year / month / season)
- **DV:** `ABCDE`

**Wrangling Plan:** ABCDE

**Visualization Plan:** ABCDE


---

## RO3 — Global Reach & Language Diversity {.smaller}

**Jungmin Woo**

**Objective & Problem Definition**

- **Objective:** Evaluate whether a movie's original language impacts its global popularity (`Popularity`) and viewer engagement (`Vote_Count`)
- **Research Question:** Do non-English movies achieve comparable global popularity and public traction compared to English-dominated films in this dataset?
- **Why it matters:** In the era of global streaming platforms (Netflix, international cinema), understanding the reach of non-English content helps distribution teams and OTT platforms allocate localization budgets (dubbing/subtitling) effectively

---

**Variables**

- **IV:** `Original_Language` (categorical)
- **DV:** `Popularity` (continuous), `Vote_Count` (continuous — volume of audience engagement)

---

**Wrangling Plan**

- Recode `Original_Language` into English (`en`) vs. Non-English (Others), or isolate the top 5 most frequent non-English languages (e.g., Spanish, Japanese, French, Korean) to avoid high-cardinality noise
- Handle outliers: apply log transformation to `Vote_Count` if heavily right-skewed
- Check for and remove missing values in the language column

---

**Visualization Plan**

- **Distribution comparison:** density plot or box plot comparing `Popularity` between English vs. Non-English movies
- **Engagement analysis:** grouped bar chart of average `Vote_Count` across the top 5 non-English languages vs. English


---

## RO4 — ABCDE {.smaller}

**(Team Member D)**

**Research Objective:** ABCDE

- **IV:** `ABCDE` word count (or presence of key keywords)
- **DV:** `ABCDE`

**Wrangling Plan:** ABCDE

**Visualization Plan:** ABCDE

---

## RO5 — ABCDE {.smaller}

**(Team Member E)**

**Research Objective:** ABCDE

- **IV:** `ABCDE` (binned into engagement tiers: Low / Medium / High)
- **DV:** `ABCDE`

**Wrangling Plan:** ABCDE


**Visualization Plan:** ABCDE

---

## Conclusion & Future Scope

**Summary**

- This proposal analyzes how genre, timing, language, textual features, and audience engagement jointly shape a movie's success

**Expected Impact**

- Final analysis (Ch. 4–5) will translate into data-driven content production and scheduling recommendations for studios and streaming platforms

---

## Q&A

### Thank you. Any questions?
